title: Le passé composé avec être
description: Past tense for movement and state verbs that take être instead of avoir, with past-participle agreement for the subject.

cards.notes: Takes **être** in the passé composé. Irregular past participle: **allé**. *Je suis allé(e)* — I went.
Takes **être**. Past participle: **venu**. *Elle est venue* — she came.
Takes **être**. Past participle: **arrivé**. *Nous sommes arrivés* — we arrived.
Takes **être**. Past participle: **parti**. *Ils sont partis* — they left.
A **state** verb — still takes **être**. Past participle: **resté**. *Il est resté* — he stayed.
Takes **être**. Past participle: **tombé**. *Elle est tombée* — she fell.
Takes **être**. Past participle: **entré**. *Je suis entré(e)* — I went in.
Takes **être**. Past participle: **sorti**. *Nous sommes sortis* — we went out.
Takes **être**. Past participle: **rentré**. *Elle est rentrée* — she came home.
Takes **être**. Past participle: **monté**. *Ils sont montés* — they went up.
Takes **être**. Irregular past participle: **descendu**. *Elle est descendue* — she went down.
Takes **être**. Irregular past participle: **né**. *Je suis né(e) en 2000* — I was born in 2000.

steps.title: A small group uses être
steps.body:
# Le passé composé avec être

In lesson 10 you built the passé composé with **avoir** + past participle: *J'ai mangé*, *Elle a regardé*. That covers most verbs.

But a **small, fixed group** uses **être** instead of avoir. These are mostly verbs of **movement** (coming, going, arriving, leaving) and a few of **state** (staying, being born).

> **être** (conjugated) + **past participle**

- *Je **suis allé** au marché.* — I went to the market.
- *Elle **est venue** hier.* — She came yesterday.
- *Nous **sommes arrivés** ce matin.* — We arrived this morning.

Teachers call this group **la maison d'être** (the "house of être"): **aller, venir, arriver, partir, entrer, sortir, monter, descendre, rester, tomber, naître, mourir**. Most come in opposite pairs (go up / go down, arrive / leave, enter / exit), which makes them easier to remember.

This lesson focuses on getting comfortable **producing** these forms yourself.

exercise.prompt: Match each être-verb with its English meaning.
Match each past participle with its infinitive.

steps.title: The participle agrees with the subject
steps.body:
## Agreement: the big difference from avoir

With **être**, the past participle **agrees** with the subject — exactly like an adjective.

- **Feminine** subject: add **-e**
- **Plural** subject: add **-s**
- **Feminine plural**: add **-es**

Using **aller** (allé):

| Subject | Form | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| il | il est **allé** | he went |
| elle | elle est **allée** | she went |
| ils (masc.) | ils sont **allés** | they went |
| elles (fem.) | elles sont **allées** | they went |

All four sound the **same** when spoken — the agreement only shows in **spelling**. But spelling it correctly matters in writing.

*Nous sommes arrivés* (a mixed or male group), *nous sommes arrivées* (an all-female group).

exercise.prompt: Translate: *She went to Paris.* (Use être. Watch the feminine agreement on the participle.)
Arrange to mean: *I arrived yesterday.* (masculine speaker)
exercise.hint: Subject + form of *être* + participle + time marker.
*partir* takes *être*; *elles* is feminine plural, so add **-es** to the participle.

steps.title: Avoir vs être side by side
steps.body:
## Choosing the right auxiliary

Most verbs take **avoir** (lesson 10). Only the small être-group takes **être**. Compare:

| With avoir (no agreement) | With être (agreement) |
|---|---|
| J'**ai** mangé — I ate | Je **suis** allé(e) — I went |
| Elle **a** regardé — she watched | Elle **est** venue — she came |
| Ils **ont** travaillé — they worked | Ils **sont** sortis — they went out |

Two things change with être:

1. The **auxiliary** is *suis / es / est / sommes / êtes / sont* instead of *ai / as / a / avons / avez / ont*.
2. The **participle agrees** with the subject (-e, -s, -es).

> Quick test: is the verb about **going / coming / moving / staying / being born**? If yes, it probably takes **être**. Everything else takes **avoir**.

- *J'ai mangé* (avoir — eating is not movement)
- *Je suis allé* (être — going is movement)

accept: Il est resté à la maison.
il est resté à la maison
Il est reste a la maison.
exercise.hint: *Il est* + *resté* + *à la maison*. Masculine subject = no extra ending.
distractors: Il a resté à la maison.
Il est restée à la maison.
